© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Key Takeaways:
• After learning the basics of the Quick Launch, I’m now going to walk you through three
different examples
• Example 1: Jeff’s “Tax Sale” - this classic example can be used around any event in your
life. Another PLF owner used a story about a pipe bursting in his home
• Timing
o Super quick with very little effort
o Two days - four emails and a landing page
• Offer - discount off normal price
o Reason why
o Story
o Scarcity
o Advanced: “Future Pacing” - a copy technique
• I had an internal memo to my partner explaining the tax bill surprise. I mentioned that
we should use this as an opportunity to offer a sale and tell them exactly why
• Email 1 - April 14th: went out 36-48 hours after I came up with the idea
o Copy in the email should be very conversational
o Triggers: story based, reason why, scarcity
o Send the prospect straight to the order page right off a “reason why” email
o At the end I tied it back to the topic of the market we were selling into
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• Email 2 - April 15th
o Hitting them with more scarcity, at this point there was only one day left
• Email 3 - April 16th
o I basically did the same thing again
o “You should have jumped on board because you would have made some money
already”
o Big time scarcity, only 8 hours left - sent them directly to landing page
• Email 4 - April 16th (later in the day)
o Although this is the 4th email in the series it’s only the 2nd dedicated email
o Here I used the “reluctant hero” strategy by saying I’m sending this final email
reminder so I don’t get the angry responses by people who missed the sale
o “Future pacing”: get people to walk forward in time and imagine the negative
place they would be if they didn’t purchase from us - missing out on the
opportunity
• Sales page - plain and simple
o Repeat the story
o Repeat the offer
o Repeat scarcity
o Show testimonials
• In the end, any unexpected thing that went wrong is a good story – however you never
want to be begging. Use humor and it can be very effective, all without hurting perceived
value. Enjoy!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Let’s walk through the Quick Launch.
I have three examples I’m going to walk you through. They’re very different
examples, but you’ll see how the Quick Launch can work in a lot of different ways.
The first one is this classic tax sale. I did this years and years ago, but it keeps on
working. I just heard from two Product Launch Formula owners that have done almost
this exact same thing in the last couple of months.
I did it as a tax sale, you can go ahead and use this example to do it as a tax sale
yourself. But you could do it around any event in your life. One Product Launch Formula
owner did it as, “My pipes burst.” He went away on vacation, when he came back the
pipes in his house had burst and he needed to make a lot of money to pay for the
plumber to come in and fix it. You can use it in a lot of different ways, the tax sale is just
one example.
I’m going to walk you through this and you’re going to see the timing was very
quick and it required very little effort. It was basically four emails and a landing page. I
came up with the idea, I implemented it the very next day, and it was ultra quick.
The offer in this case was that I gave a discount off my normal price. The triggers
I hit were the reason why, a story, scarcity, and I had an advanced on that I haven’t
mentioned so far in the course yet that we call future pacing. That’s not really a trigger,
it’s more like a copy technique. That’s sort of the background on the tax sale. Let’s get
right to exactly how I did it.
The fun thing about this is I actually captured some of my thinking in this
internal memo. This was not a marketing piece; this was basically an email I sent to my
partner in this business explaining this idea that I had. I actually sent this on April 12th
and you’ll see we started the sale on April 14th, so it took all of two days. I was telling my
partner that I got this nasty surprise on my taxes and I need to come up with $25,000.
Not that I needed to come up with it, but that I would like to come up with it. I said,
“Let’s use it as a reason why.” You can basically see my thinking here and this was
literally on the afternoon of April 12th and you’ll see it didn’t take too long to put together
the tax sale.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
This was the first email I sent out on the morning of April 14th, so it was about 36
to 48 hours after I came up with the idea. These are the triggers I hit here in this email;
story, reason why, and scarcity.
Subject line: Jeff’s Huge Tax Bill
It was just very conversational. You’ll notice my copy is always very conversation.
I tell them it’s a little embarrassing, I got a nasty surprise and I’m going to have write a
big check. This is all just story based stuff.
More story based stuff here.
Here, bottom line, I’m putting this on sale, it’s an IRS sale, we don’t normally
discount trading subscriptions but now we’re going to offer a discount.
Here I’m already started to build scarcity. It’s April 14th and I’m sending this to
them and they only have until April 16th.
Basically here I explain the offer and then I send them right to the order page. In
this case, there wasn’t any Prelaunch at all, we took them straight to an order page right
off a reason why email.
Here this is sort of talking about the current market. This is for the stock market
traders, but up here it was completely 100% all about this story. Remember those
triggers I’m hitting; the story, the reason why, and the scarcity. Here we tie it a little bit
back to the topic for the market that we’re selling into, but you can see the pitch is mostly
about the story.
Here telling them this is the best price ever. That’s sort of setting the hook on the
scarcity.
Here’s the second email that we sent out. One of the things that we used to do is
each day we would send this update out. This update would go out the day after we
published it to our real time traders. That was the way we marketed.
Every day we would publish this update to our paid subscribers and then the next
day we would send this out to our prospect list. It was the exact same email, but I would
have a built-in marketing message and I would put it up at the top here and I would put
in the middle. This is how we marketed every single day, so every day they got an email
that we had sent out the night before to our paid subscribers.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
One thing I want to mention is this red in here is just for your benefit so you
could see this, so that it stands out a little bit. When we actually published this it was not
in red, it was just pure email.
At the top of the message I just said, “sale, sale, sale,” and we’re already hitting
them with scarcity, “There’s just one day left.”
You can see this is a long time ago, but this technique still works. Like I said, in
the last couple of months I’ve had two Product Launch Formula owners that basically
used this exact same campaign and had it work for them. This technique is going to work
30 years from now.
We came down here, we recapped how we did in this update, and then basically
it’s scarcity, “Just 24 hours left,” referring to the IRS sale, bringing that story back in.
This was sent April 14th...This is sent April 15th...Now this goes out April 16th, very
similar to what we did the day before. In fact, it’s almost identical. The only thing I did
was I really changed this top part. Instead of “sale, sale, sale,” it said, “IRS sale.”
Came down here and basically did the same thing. This was, “Boy, you should
have jumped on board, because you would have made some money already.”
Then we came down to big time scarcity, “We’re down to eight hours left.” We’re
sending them directly to the landing page.
Then on the same day, April 16th, we came back later on and this was a dedicated
email. These previous two emails went out as part of our normal marketing message, this
was another dedicated email. This was only the second dedicated email. We sent one out
on the 14th. This is the fourth email in the series, but it’s only the second dedicated email.
This one came out just a few hours after the third one.
Here’s the fourth email. We haven’t talked about this in the course yet, but we
will. This is a bit of the reluctant hero. I’m getting angry email from someone who missed
the deadline for our IRS sale. This is a little of that future pacing, this is where we try to
get people to walk forward in time and imagine where they would be if they didn’t
purchase from us and the negatives to that.
This is basically, “If we don’t give you that final warning, someone is going to
miss out on the savings and they’re going to get really torque off, and then I’m going to
get a nasty email. This way I send a final warning and I’m covered.” That’s a bit of future
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 7 --

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
pacing in there. That can be a really powerful technique. It’s sort of an advanced copy
technique where you’re just walking someone forward and showing them the
consequences if they don’t take any action.
Scarcity, scarcity, scarcity. “You just have eight hours. You’ve already heard my
IRS saga.” Even though I’m telling them I’m not going to repeat it, by referring back to it
I am tapping into the story and the reason why again.
Then we go down and I talk about the guarantee. That’s email number four. Here
I want to show you the landing page that we would drop people on.
It’s very simple, it’s just IRS Sale and playing into the story, “Jeff screwed up his
tax planning and he needs to raise cash fast, so he’s putting this real time updates on sale
for ridiculous prices.” I just repeat the story here, repeat the offer, repeat the scarcity. We
come down and we give them a bunch of testimonials. That’s about it.
This is incredibly simple promotion. We just had the original reason why, a
couple of reminders, then a final scarcity message, and we dropped them on this super
simple landing page. This landing page was just I took my normal order page and I put
up some additional text. Total time invested in this is probably two or three hours and we
made a boatload of money, it was very effective.
Like I mentioned, it has been used both in this form as a tax sale and it’s been
used recently with great success by my Product Launch Formula owners. Also, you can
use it as a “my pipes burst” sale, or any type of thing that went wrong, some unexpected
thing that went wrong.
You want to avoid looking like it’s a begathon. You never want to be begging,
that’s not an attractive thing to do. If you can use a little bit of humor, like I did here, I
wasn’t begging at all. I threw in, “I screwed up,” and a little bit of humor. This type of
sale can be very effective and you can do it without hurting the perceived value of your
offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
